# Feature Specification: M0.3 -- NPC Talks
*Generated by Game Studio Sub-Agents v1.0*

## Feature Overview
**Feature Name**: AI-Powered NPC Dialogue System
**Project**: Nookstead
**Engine**: Phaser.js 3 + Next.js 16 + Colyseus + Claude API
**Priority**: High (Core USP -- this is the reason the game exists)
**Estimated Effort**: 2 weeks (10 working days)
**Dependencies**: M0.1 (Walking on Tiles), M0.2 (Multiplayer Sync)
**Owner**: Mechanics Developer + Sr Game Designer
**Data Impact**: npc_dialogue_start, npc_dialogue_turn, npc_dialogue_end (AI cost tracking is critical here)

## Market Justification
**Player Demand**: This is Nookstead's unique selling proposition. Character.AI has 20M+ MAU proving massive demand for AI-powered conversation. "NPC dialogue gets repetitive" is the #1 complaint in farming game communities.
**Competitive Analysis**: Zero competitors have AI-generated NPC dialogue with personality persistence. Stardew Valley has ~30 unique lines per NPC per relationship level. Animal Crossing has slightly more variety but still finite. Nookstead offers infinite, contextual dialogue.
**Market Impact**: This single feature justifies the entire project. If it feels magical, everything else (farming, building) becomes differentiated by its context. If it feels generic, the project has no USP.
**Priority Rationale**: Third milestone because it requires working multiplayer (NPC is a server entity) and working movement (player approaches NPC). This is the make-or-break feature.

## Problem Statement
When a player approaches an NPC and presses the interact key, a dialogue window opens. The NPC responds using Claude API, generating contextual responses based on its seed persona (personality, profession, traits, speech style). The player can respond via quick replies (pre-generated contextual options) or free text. The conversation is limited to 10 turns.

## User Stories
- **As a player**, I want to **talk to an NPC and receive a response that feels like a real conversation** so that **I feel a genuine connection to the character**
- **As a player**, I want to **choose from suggested quick replies OR type my own message** so that **I have both convenience and freedom**
- **As a player**, I want to **see the NPC's portrait animate during dialogue** so that **the conversation feels alive and expressive**
- **As a player**, I want **the NPC to stay in character** so that **immersion is never broken**

## Acceptance Criteria
### Must Have
- [ ] Player presses E/taps near NPC to initiate dialogue
- [ ] Dialogue UI window opens: NPC portrait (left), text area (right), input area (bottom)
- [ ] NPC generates response via Claude API based on seed persona
- [ ] Response appears within 3 seconds (LLM latency target)
- [ ] NPC stays in character (personality, speech style consistent with seed persona)
- [ ] 3-4 quick reply options generated contextually
- [ ] Free text input available via "Say your own" button
- [ ] Conversation limited to 10 turns (5 player, 5 NPC)
- [ ] NPC says goodbye naturally when turn limit reached
- [ ] At least 1 fully configured NPC: Marko the baker
- [ ] LLM response includes emotion metadata (agree/disagree/neutral)

### Should Have
- [ ] NPC portrait animation: Talk (when responding), Nod (agree), Shake (disagree)
- [ ] Typing indicator while waiting for LLM response
- [ ] Free text input moderation (basic toxicity filter)
- [ ] Dialogue log (scrollable history of current conversation)
- [ ] NPC speaks differently based on time of day and current activity

### Could Have
- [ ] Speech bubble above NPC head showing shortened response
- [ ] Multiple NPC seed personas configured (Marko, Anna, Mayor Viktor)
- [ ] NPC-to-NPC conversations visible to nearby players

## Functional Requirements

### Dialogue Flow
```
1. Player within 2 tiles of NPC + presses E/taps
2. Server validates: NPC not in dialogue with another player
3. Server marks NPC as inDialogue=true
4. Client opens DialogueWindow component
5. Server requests AI service for initial greeting
6. AI service: seed_persona + time_of_day + NPC_current_action -> Claude API
7. Response returned: { text, emotion, quick_replies: string[3-4] }
8. Client displays NPC greeting + quick reply buttons + free text option
9. Player selects quick reply OR types free text
10. Client sends player message to server
11. Server forwards to AI service with full context
12. AI service: seed_persona + conversation_history + player_message -> Claude API
13. Response returned, displayed. Repeat for up to 10 turns.
14. On turn limit: AI generates farewell message. DialogueWindow closes.
15. Server marks NPC as inDialogue=false
```

### Seed Persona Format (Marko the Baker)
```json
{
  "name": "Marko",
  "age": 38,
  "profession": "Baker",
  "traits": ["extrovert", "generous", "impulsive", "creative", "anxious"],
  "bio": "Marko moved to Quiet Haven 5 years ago from the big city where he worked in a restaurant. He always dreamed of his own bakery. He opened a shop on Trade Street and takes pride in every loaf. He fears his business won't survive if competition comes.",
  "interests": ["baking", "football", "loud music"],
  "fears": ["bankruptcy", "loneliness"],
  "goals": ["expand the bakery", "find an apprentice"],
  "speech_style": "Loud, emotional, uses exclamations. Often offers fresh bread to taste.",
  "current_role_in_town": "Owns the bakery on Trade Street. Opens at 7:30, closes at 18:00. Lunches at the cafe. Evenings at the bar."
}
```

### System Prompt Template
```
You are {name}, a {age}-year-old {profession} living in Quiet Haven.

Personality: {traits_description}
Background: {bio}
Speech style: {speech_style}

Current situation: It is {time} on {day}, {season}. You are currently {current_action} at {location}.

RULES:
- Always stay in character as {name}
- Respond in 1-3 sentences (keep it concise for a game dialogue)
- Match the speech style described above
- Never break the fourth wall or mention being an AI
- Never discuss topics outside what {name} would know about
- If the player is rude, respond as {name} would (hurt, confused, annoyed -- not as a language model)

Respond with JSON:
{
  "text": "your response",
  "emotion": "agree|disagree|neutral|happy|sad|surprised",
  "quick_replies": ["option 1", "option 2", "option 3"]
}
```

### Input Specifications
- **Interact**: E key (desktop) or tap on NPC sprite (mobile) within 2-tile radius
- **Quick Reply**: Click/tap on reply button
- **Free Text**: Click "Say your own" -> text field appears -> type -> press Enter/Send
- **Close Dialogue**: Press ESC or click X button (closes and NPC says abbreviated farewell)

### Output Specifications
- **Visual**: Dialogue window overlay. NPC portrait with emotion-driven animation. Text with typewriter effect (50ms per character). Quick reply buttons below text.
- **Audio**: NPC "voice" sound effect (mumble/chirp per character, like Animal Crossing). UI click sounds for buttons.

## Technical Requirements
### AI Service Integration
```typescript
// POST /agent/dialogue
interface DialogueRequest {
  agent_id: string;        // 'marko_001'
  player_id: string;
  player_message: string;
  conversation_history: { role: 'player' | 'npc'; text: string }[];
  context: {
    time_of_day: string;   // '14:30'
    day: number;
    season: string;
    weather: string;
    npc_current_action: string;
    npc_location: string;
  };
}

interface DialogueResponse {
  text: string;
  emotion: 'agree' | 'disagree' | 'neutral' | 'happy' | 'sad' | 'surprised';
  quick_replies: string[];
  llm_model: string;       // 'sonnet' or 'haiku'
  llm_latency_ms: number;
  llm_tokens_in: number;
  llm_tokens_out: number;
  llm_cost_usd: number;
}
```

### Performance Constraints
- **LLM Latency**: < 3 seconds for full dialogue response
- **Token Budget**: < 2000 tokens per request (input + output)
- **Cost**: < $0.005 per dialogue turn (Sonnet pricing)
- **Memory**: Conversation history kept in-memory for current dialogue session only (no persistence yet -- that is M0.4)

### UI Component (React Overlay)
```typescript
// Rendered as absolute-positioned React component over Phaser canvas
interface DialogueWindowProps {
  npcName: string;
  npcPortrait: string;          // Sprite sheet reference
  npcEmotion: string;
  messages: { role: 'player' | 'npc'; text: string }[];
  quickReplies: string[];
  isLoading: boolean;           // Show typing indicator
  turnCount: number;
  maxTurns: number;
  onSendMessage: (text: string) => void;
  onSelectReply: (reply: string) => void;
  onClose: () => void;
}
```

## Balancing Parameters
- **Max Dialogue Turns**: 10 - Range: 6-15 - Purpose: Controls session length and API cost
- **Max Response Length**: 150 characters - Range: 80-250 - Purpose: Keeps responses game-appropriate (not essay-length)
- **Quick Reply Count**: 3-4 - Range: 2-5 - Purpose: Balance between choice and cognitive load
- **Interaction Radius**: 2 tiles (32px) - Range: 1-3 tiles - Purpose: How close player must be to NPC
- **Typewriter Speed**: 50ms/char - Range: 20-100 - Purpose: Reading pace comfort

## Edge Cases & Error Handling
- **NPC already in dialogue**: Show speech bubble "I'm busy talking to someone" -- player cannot initiate
- **LLM timeout (>5s)**: Show "thinking..." for 5s, then generic fallback response from cache: "Sorry, my mind wandered. What were you saying?"
- **LLM error/failure**: Use cached greeting. Log error. Retry on next turn.
- **Toxic player input**: Pre-filter with keyword list. If detected, NPC responds: "[Name] looks uncomfortable and changes the subject."
- **Player walks away during dialogue**: Dialogue auto-closes. NPC says abbreviated farewell.
- **Multiple players approach same NPC**: First player gets dialogue. Others see "NPC is talking to someone."

## Analytics Integration
### Telemetry Events
- **npc_dialogue_start**: NPC id, player id, zone, relationship level, NPC action/emotion
- **npc_dialogue_turn**: Turn number, input type (quick/free), message lengths, LLM model, latency, tokens, cost, cache hit
- **npc_dialogue_end**: Total turns, total duration, total LLM cost, end reason

### Key Metrics to Watch
- **Dialogue depth**: Are players using all 10 turns or leaving after 2-3?
- **Free text vs quick reply ratio**: Which input method is preferred?
- **LLM cost per dialogue**: Must stay under $0.05 per full conversation
- **Latency p95**: Must stay under 5 seconds
- **Character consistency**: Manual review of NPC responses for quality (pre-launch)

## Testing Plan
### Unit Tests
- [ ] Seed persona correctly injected into system prompt
- [ ] Conversation history correctly formatted for Claude API
- [ ] Emotion metadata correctly parsed from LLM response
- [ ] Turn counter correctly limits dialogue to max turns
- [ ] Toxicity filter catches common offensive patterns

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Full flow: player interacts -> server -> AI service -> Claude API -> client displays response
- [ ] Quick replies are contextually relevant to conversation
- [ ] Dialogue state correctly tracked across multiple turns
- [ ] NPC inDialogue flag prevents concurrent dialogues

### Player Tests
- [ ] NPC "feels like a real character" (qualitative assessment)
- [ ] Responses are consistent with seed persona personality
- [ ] Dialogue UI is easy to read and interact with on desktop and mobile
- [ ] Players want to talk to NPCs more than once (engagement signal)

### Performance Tests
- [ ] LLM response latency < 3 seconds (p50), < 5 seconds (p95)
- [ ] LLM cost per full dialogue (10 turns) < $0.05
- [ ] Dialogue UI does not cause FPS drop (Phaser keeps running underneath)
